listeners.note: NOT applicable — the branch module intentionally ships NOTHING on TenantProvisioned. Branches are explicit tenant-admin onboarding actions (see readme §2). The tenant admin creates the first Branch(es) via the /branches/create screen after tenant provisioning completes.

listeners.purpose: Fires when a Tenant is being erased. Iterates every Branch on the tenant: cancels any in-flight `GeocodeAddressJob` for those branches (avoids wasted subprocessor spend after the row is gone) + emits a per-branch metric so the platform can graph erasure fanout. The actual DELETE cascades via the DB FK (`branches.tenant_id ON DELETE CASCADE`) — this listener runs BEFORE the cascade to do the job-cancellation cleanup.

Fires when the Organization module (a sibling) archives an Organization. Verifies that every Branch under the archived org is already closed OR under-migration; refuses the org archival otherwise via the shared archival-precondition contract. Belt-and-braces on top of the Organization module's own checks.
Same shape as OnOrganizationArchived — verifies no active Branch references the region before allowing region archival.

Wave 3 hand-off. Fires when the geofencing module (once landed) updates a polygon attached to a Branch. Invalidates the `br:coverage:{branch_id}` cache + fires an activity_log entry naming the change. No-op in Wave 2 (the geofencing module doesn't ship yet); listener is registered lazily via the discovery scan when geofencing appears.

Fires when any of the 5 branch entitlements changes for a tenant (branch_slot / branch_geocoding / branch_capacity_gating / branch_amenities / branch_multi_period_hours). Reconciles the tenant's state — e.g. when `branch_slot` decreases below the current active-branch count on downgrade, the reconciler picks the newest-created non-default branches and freezes them (status=paused with reason='entitlement_downgrade'). See the entitlements-reconciler service for the full policy.
